title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Language Clinic: User Decision
summary: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the user decision increment in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter…
objectives: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-user-decision — Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_outcomes_repo_evidence_3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Build User Decision evidence · Theory User Decision
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-user-decision-noj4nr" that uses case 8516 to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Build User Decision evidence · Theory User Decision.

For Bookbot and Python Build User Decision evidence · Theory User Decision, keep User Decision connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-user-decision-noj4nr
# Competency: bookbot-user-decision.
# Case 8516: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_user_decision_noj4nr(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_noj4nr = "noj4nr-rn4jon-8516"
    assert evidence_variant_noj4nr
    evidence_axes_noj4nr = (
        "corpusnoj4nr",
        "policyrn4jon",
        "revision8516",
        "commandnoj4nr8516",
        "reportrn4jon8516",
        "test8516noj4nr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_noj4nr)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the User Decision model · Theory User Decision, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, User Decision precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the User Decision model · Theory User Decision, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6386 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the User Decision model · Theory User Decision, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Printing any word total is a complete Bookbot outcome.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-user-decision-noj4nr.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-user-decision-noj4nr(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_user_decision_noj4nr\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not user\.strip)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not authorized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not repository_clean)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not changed_case_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using report and user plus specific case evidence.
